title: Multi-User Laboratory for Green Hydrogen Studies
description: A shared experimental and computational facility under the National Institute of Science and Technology in Energy Process Control and Automation, supporting electrolysis modeling, control of hydrogen production cells, and benchmarking of control strategies for green hydrogen systems across partner institutions.
funding: National Institute of Science and Technology in Energy Process Control and Automation (INCT)
collaborators: Partner institutions of the INCT network

title: Learning-Based Optimization and Control for Complex Real-World Systems
description: Combining machine learning with model-based control to handle systems that are too complex or uncertain for purely analytical design, including neural-operator approximations of control kernels, learned reduced-order models, and data-driven constraint tightening for predictive control.

title: Control and Optimization of Energy Generation, Utilization, and Storage Systems
description: Methods for the coordinated control of energy generation, consumption, and storage, with an emphasis on renewable sources such as solar and wind. The project addresses optimal dispatch, storage management, and robustness to the variability inherent to renewable generation.

title: Boundary Control Kernels for Coupled Reaction–Diffusion Systems
description: A unified backstepping framework for exponentially stabilizing coupled parabolic PDEs with spatially varying coefficients. The project delivers well-posedness proofs for the control-kernel equations together with an open-source solver and reproducible numerical benchmarks.

title: Data-Driven Stabilization of Thermoacoustic Instabilities
description: Reduced-order modeling and control of a Rijke-tube thermoacoustic system using dynamic mode decomposition with control. The work establishes practical exponential stability on a finite-dimensional product space and validates the controller on an experimental rig.
